entities.name: BIOLOGICAL COMPLEXITY
entities.description: The state of biological systems characterized by non-linear interactions and entanglement

entities.name: NON-LINEAR INTERACTIONS
entities.description: Interactions that do not have a proportional relationship between input and output, contributing to biological complexity

entities.name: GENES
entities.description: Biological entities that are part of the interactions within complex adaptive systems

entities.description: Biological entities that are part of the interactions within complex adaptive systems

entities.description: Biological entities that are part of the interactions within complex adaptive systems

entities.name: COMPLEX ADAPTIVE SYSTEMS
entities.description: Systems showing features like self-organisation, modularity, and adaptation

entities.description: A characteristic feature of complex adaptive systems

entities.name: MODULARITY
entities.description: A characteristic feature of complex adaptive systems

entities.name: EMERGENCE
entities.description: A characteristic feature of complex adaptive systems

entities.name: COLLECTIVE RESPONSE
entities.description: A characteristic feature of complex adaptive systems

entities.name: ADAPTATION
entities.description: A characteristic feature of complex adaptive systems

entities.name: THEORY OF COMPLEX NETWORKS
entities.description: A formal framework used for modelling complex biological systems to gain insights into biological complexity

entities.name: GENE FAMILY
entities.description: A group of genes related by sequence or function, representing a local scale of biological complexity

entities.description: Technologies used to generate large amounts of biological data in the genomics era

entities.name: GENOMICS
entities.description: The study of genomes, providing the data for network-theoretic investigations

entities.name: NETWORK-THEORETIC EMPIRICAL INVESTIGATIONS
entities.description: Investigations using the formal framework of complex networks to understand system complexity
